_source: TH_EXPLORE_IT_DATA.json (authoritative structured data). Real accessions, per-item licence + open_access flag. Nothing authored by an assistant.

station.tagline: He didn't just want to be money. He wanted to be the place where every deal in the world gets made.

museum_objects.title: Ethereum Whitepaper (Official)
museum_objects.description: The founding whitepaper written by a then-19-year-old Vitalik Buterin, proposing a blockchain that could run programmable 'smart contracts,' not just move currency.
museum_objects.significance: Zephyr was never content just being money — this document is the moment a teenager decided currency should be able to think, negotiate, and build entire economies on top of itself.
museum_objects.license: Ethereum Foundation-hosted, freely quotable with attribution
museum_objects.plain_language: The original document, written by a 19-year-old, that proposed a new kind of currency that could also run computer programs called 'smart contracts.'
museum_objects.licence: Ethereum Foundation-hosted, freely quotable with attribution

resolved.via: long tail — needs a per-domain licence rule

museum_objects.rights_note: NO OPEN IMAGE FOUND — panel must show text/citation only.

museum_objects.title: Devcon 0 Archive (Berlin, November 2014)
museum_objects.description: Footage and records from Ethereum's first-ever developer conference, held in Berlin, including Gavin Wood's welcome address before the network had even launched.
museum_objects.significance: This is Zephyr's first public gathering — a room full of strangers betting on an idea that didn't technically exist yet.
museum_objects.license: Ethereum Foundation content / Standard YouTube license
museum_objects.plain_language: Video and records from Ethereum's very first conference in Berlin in 2014, held a year before the network officially launched.
museum_objects.licence: Ethereum Foundation content / Standard YouTube license

resolved.via: long tail — needs a per-domain licence rule

museum_objects.rights_note: NO OPEN IMAGE FOUND — panel must show text/citation only.

museum_objects.title: 'Hard Fork Completed' Blog Post — The DAO Crisis
museum_objects.description: Official Ethereum Foundation announcement confirming the controversial hard fork executed at block 1,920,000 to reverse The DAO hack, which had drained 3.64 million ETH.
museum_objects.significance: This is Zephyr's identity crisis laid bare — when code got hacked for $50-60 million, the community broke in two over whether rules should ever bend, even to fix a theft.
museum_objects.plain_language: An official 2016 announcement explaining how Ethereum's community made the controversial choice to reverse a huge hack by changing the rules of the network itself.

resolved.via: long tail — needs a per-domain licence rule

display_alternatives.title: 6 of Tethers, from The Cloisters Playing Cards

museum_objects.rights_note: NO OPEN IMAGE FOUND — panel must show text/citation only.

museum_objects.title: The Merge Announcement
museum_objects.description: Official record of Ethereum's transition from energy-intensive proof-of-work to proof-of-stake, cutting energy consumption by 99.95%.
museum_objects.significance: After years of being criticized for burning electricity like an old furnace, Zephyr rebuilt his own engine mid-flight and came out cleaner than anyone thought possible.
museum_objects.plain_language: The official record of the moment in 2022 when Ethereum changed its entire technical engine to use 99.95% less electricity.

resolved.via: long tail — needs a per-domain licence rule

museum_objects.rights_note: NO OPEN IMAGE FOUND — panel must show text/citation only.

museum_objects.title: Vitalik Buterin, TechCrunch London 2015 (Photograph)
museum_objects.description: A photograph of Ethereum co-founder Vitalik Buterin at a TechCrunch conference in London, taken shortly after Ethereum's mainnet launch.
museum_objects.significance: This is the only face anyone ever really associates with Zephyr — a 21-year-old who looked far too young to be reinventing what money could do.
museum_objects.license: CC BY 2.0 (verify exact file page license)
museum_objects.plain_language: A photo of Ethereum's young co-founder taken at a London tech conference in 2015, shortly after the network went live.

image_qa.what_i_see: A man with short brown hair and a green shirt, wearing a microphone, in front of a 'TechCrunch' backdrop.
image_qa.why: The image matches the description of Vitalik Buterin at a TechCrunch conference, as it shows a man in a setting consistent with the claim.

primary_sources.author: Vitalik Buterin
primary_sources.description: The original proposal for a blockchain supporting programmable smart contracts.
primary_sources.license: Ethereum Foundation content, freely quotable with attribution
primary_sources.plain_language: The founding document explaining how Ethereum would let people build programs directly into a currency.

primary_sources.title: Wikipedia: The DAO
primary_sources.author: Wikipedia contributors
primary_sources.description: Detailed account of The DAO's May 2016 launch, its June 2016 hack, and the resulting hard fork.
primary_sources.plain_language: The full story of a 2016 hack that drained millions of dollars in Ethereum and forced the community to change the network's rules.

primary_sources.author: Ethereum Foundation
primary_sources.description: Official announcement detailing the technical mechanics and timeline of The Merge.
primary_sources.plain_language: An official announcement explaining exactly how and when Ethereum switched to a much more energy-efficient system in 2022.

primary_sources.title: Wikipedia: Ethereum Classic
primary_sources.author: Wikipedia contributors
primary_sources.description: Explains the split between Ethereum and Ethereum Classic following The DAO hard fork, and the 'code is law' philosophical debate.
primary_sources.plain_language: The story of how Ethereum split into two separate currencies after a disagreement about whether a hack should ever be reversed.

primary_sources.title: Statista: DeFi Total Value Locked Across Blockchains
primary_sources.author: Statista
primary_sources.description: Data tracking the growth of decentralized finance (DeFi) total value locked from roughly $1 billion to $100 billion during 2020-2021.
primary_sources.license: Statista — free for citation, paid license required for chart reuse
primary_sources.plain_language: Data showing how the amount of money locked into Ethereum-based financial apps grew from $1 billion to $100 billion in about a year.
primary_sources.licence: Statista — free for citation, paid license required for chart reuse

world_map.map_description: Zephyr was conceived on internet forums, formally introduced to developers in Berlin, launched globally, and now operates through a foundation based in Switzerland.

nodes.label: Berlin, Germany (Devcon 0)
nodes.description: Site of Ethereum's first developer conference in November 2014, before the network had even launched.

nodes.label: Zug, Switzerland (Ethereum Foundation)
nodes.description: Home of the Ethereum Foundation, the nonprofit organization supporting Ethereum's development, located in Switzerland's 'Crypto Valley.'

nodes.label: Global Mainnet Network ('Frontier')
nodes.description: Ethereum's mainnet, launched July 30, 2015, runs on a decentralized global network of nodes with no single headquarters.

nodes.label: Bogotá, Colombia (Devcon VI, 2022)
nodes.description: Host city for Devcon VI in October 2022, held shortly after The Merge.

nodes.label: Bangkok, Thailand (Devcon SEA, 2024)
nodes.description: Host city for the 2024 Devcon Southeast Asia event, reflecting Ethereum's continually shifting, borderless community.
